Container.Name: HealthLog
Container.Overview:
Self-hosted personal health tracker (PWA + native iOS client): weight, blood pressure, pulse, blood glucose, body composition, sleep, mood, menstrual cycle, and medications on one encrypted timeline. AES-256-GCM at rest, no vendor cloud, no telemetry.
REQUIRES a separate PostgreSQL 16 container — install one first (CA has community PostgreSQL templates), create a database and user, then set DATABASE_URL below. Step-by-step guide: https://github.com/MBombeck/HealthLog/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/unraid.md
Generate each required secret with: openssl rand -hex 32
LAN / plain-HTTP installs MUST keep SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE set to false, otherwise the session cookie carries the Secure flag, the browser drops it over HTTP, and login silently fails.

Config.Name: DATABASE_URL
Config.Description: Connection string for your separately-installed PostgreSQL 16. Replace CHANGE-ME with the DB password and POSTGRES-HOST with the Postgres container IP or name.

Config.Name: ENCRYPTION_KEY
Config.Description: AES-256-GCM key for data at rest. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32 (64-char hex). Back this up — losing it makes encrypted data unrecoverable.

Config.Name: API_TOKEN_HMAC_KEY
Config.Description: HMAC key for API token hashing. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32 (64-char hex).

Config.Name: SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE
Config.Description: Set to false for LAN / plain-HTTP installs (http://tower:3000) or login fails. Set to true only behind an HTTPS reverse proxy.

Config.Name: NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL
Config.Description: Public URL users reach HealthLog at. Use the LAN URL (e.g. http://tower:3000) or your HTTPS domain behind a reverse proxy.

Config.Name: APP_URL
Config.Description: Internal app base URL. Normally identical to NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL.

Config.Name: ENCRYPTION_KEYS
Config.Description: Optional. Key-rotation map, e.g. {"v1":"<hex>","v2":"<hex>"}. Leave empty unless rotating; use ENCRYPTION_KEY instead.

Config.Name: ENCRYPTION_ACTIVE_KEY_ID
Config.Description: Optional. Active key id for the ENCRYPTION_KEYS map (e.g. v2). Leave empty when using ENCRYPTION_KEY.
